title:
We are given an input file named "logs/YYYY-MM-DD.log" and need to produce a output file named "logs/YYYY-MM-DD_processed.log".
The problem requires us to process the log file by filtering out lines that contain the word "error" (case-insensitive) and then sort the remaining lines alphabetically.
Additionally, we are told that if there are no lines left after filtering, we should output "No errors found." to the output file.

Steps:
1
job: End-of-day consolidation for THE MACHINE COMMAND repo. Review today's job logs in logs/. Identify completed work, key decisions, errors, and learnings. Write a consolidation report to logs/consolidations/$(date +%d%m%y)_CONSOLIDATION_1.md with sections: COMPLETED JOBS, KEY LEARNINGS, ERRORS ENCOUNTERED, FOLLOW-UP NEEDED. File naming must use DDMMYY format. Never delete files.
